paper.title: Gaia DR2 in 6D: Searching for the fastest stars in the Galaxy

review.summary: Paper publishes a 49-column distance/velocity catalogue for 7,183,262 Gaia DR2 stars with radial velocities, available at the first author's Leiden page. Contains 9 tables: 1 model parameter table (MilkyWayPotential), 1 catalogue description, 1 primary object table (20 Pub>0.8 candidates), 1 supplementary object table (105 candidates with 0.5<Pub≤0.8 across 3 continuation pages), 1 old draft table (28 candidates, unused), and 2 robustness-test tables (parallax zero-point and inflated errors). External resources: public catalogue webpage and GUMS/GOG simulation catalogues.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Defines the four-component (bulge+nucleus+disk+halo) Galactic gravitational potential used for orbit integration, escape velocity computation, and unbound probability estimation throughout the paper.

columns.name: Component
columns.meaning: Galactic component: Bulge, Nucleus, Disk, or Halo

columns.name: Parameters
columns.meaning: Mass and scale parameters for each component (Hernquist bulge/nucleus with M_i and r_i; Miyamoto-Nagai disk with M_d, a_d, b_d; NFW halo with M_h and r_s)

internal_tables.comments: Same potential as Bovy (2015). Fits the enclosed mass profile of the Milky Way.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Primary results table: observed Gaia DR2 astrometry and derived distances, Galactocentric positions, total velocities, Milky-Way origin probability, and unbound probability for the 20 high-confidence (Pub>80%) unbound star candidates.

source_refs.caption: Observed and derived properties for the $20$ "clean" high velocity star candidates with a probability $> 80\%$ of being unbound from the Galaxy. Stars are sorted by decreasing $P_\mathrm{ub}$.
source_refs.label: tab:candidates

columns.meaning: Probability that the star originates from the Milky Way stellar disk (r_min < 25 kpc)

columns.name: P_ub
columns.meaning: Probability of being unbound from the Galaxy (fraction of MC realizations exceeding escape velocity)

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Documents the 49-column schema of the publicly released distance and velocity catalogue for 7,183,262 Gaia DR2 stars with radial velocities. Includes Gaia DR2 observed quantities (cols 1-12), derived distances and Galactocentric positions (cols 13-30), and derived Galactocentric velocities (cols 31-48), plus unbound probability (col 49).

source_refs.caption: Catalogue description. Derived distances and velocities correspond to the median of the distribution, and lower and upper uncertainties are derived, respectively, from the $16$th and $84$th percentiles of the distribution function. Entries labelled $^1$ are derived in this paper, while entries labelled $^2$ are taken from the \Gaia DR2 catalogue \citep{gaiadr2}.
source_refs.label: tab:catalogue

internal_tables.evidence: Catalogue description. Derived distances and velocities correspond to the median of the distribution, and lower and upper uncertainties are derived, respectively, from the $16$th and $84$th percentiles of the distribution function. Entries labelled $^1$ are derived in this paper, while entries labelled $^2$ are taken from the \Gaia DR2 catalogue \citep{gaiadr2}.
internal_tables.comments: Catalogue available at http://home.strw.leidenuniv.nl/~marchetti/research.html. Columns 1-12 from Gaia DR2; columns 13-49 derived in this work.

external_resources.description: Public webpage hosting the full distance and velocity catalogue for 7,183,262 Gaia DR2 stars with radial velocities. 49-column catalogue described in Appendix B, Table tab:catalogue.

source_refs.context: We search for the fastest stars in the subset of stars with radial velocity measurements of the second data release (DR2) of the European Space Agency mission \Gaia. Starting from the observed positions, parallaxes, proper motions, and radial velocities, we construct the distance and total velocity distribution of more than $7$ million stars in our Milky Way, deriving the full 6D phase space information in Galactocentric coordinates. These information are shared in a catalogue, publicly available at \url{http://home.strw.leidenuniv.nl/~marchetti/research.html}.

external_resources.evidence: Abstract and Section 3: catalogue publicly available at http://home.strw.leidenuniv.nl/~marchetti/research.html
external_resources.comments: Primary data product of this paper. Also referenced at line 157 (Section 3) and line 403 (Appendix B).

external_resources.description: GUMS-18 (Gaia Universe Model Snapshot) and GOG-18 (Gaia Object Generator) simulation catalogues, used in Appendix A to calibrate the exponentially decreasing distance prior (scale length L=2600 pc) on a simulated sample of ~352,000 stars with large relative parallax errors.

source_refs.context: In this appendix we discuss the choice of the prior probability on distances $P(d)$ which gives the most accurate results on the subsample of bright stars in \Gaia DR2 with a large relative error on parallax (the \emph{high-f} sample introduced in Section \ref{sec:dist}). We cross-match the \Gaia Universe Model Snapshot \citep[GUMS,][]{robin+12} and the \Gaia Object Generator \citep[GOG,][]{luri+14} catalogues based on the value of the source identifier, to get a resulting sample of $7 \cdot 10^6$ stars with $G_\mathrm{RVS} < 12.2$. We use the latest versions of these mock catalogues, GUMS-18 and GOG-18\footnote{\url{https://wwwhip.obspm.fr/gaiasimu/}}.

external_resources.comments: External simulation data used to validate distance prior choice. Not produced by this paper.
